title: Kiln Monitor
sentToEsp: Sent to ESP32 — alarm fires once when reached.
note_bisque: Moderate ramp · slow steam release at the start
note_glaze: Fast ramp OK above 600°C · hold expected peak
note_crystalline: Very slow ramps · long holds are completely normal
note_raku: Fast ramp and rapid cooling are expected
modal_sub_day: Last activity about a day ago
modal_stored: You have {n} saved datapoints from a previous session.
modal_question: What would you like to do?
idle_body: Páramo Kiln Monitor is in deep sleep mode, so the previous firing has likely ended. Export this firing or start a new one? Starting a new firing resets stored data.
confirm_newFiring:
Start a new firing?

· All current datapoints in this browser will be deleted.
· The ESP32 will also reset its saved history.

Export first if you need the data.
confirm_clearChart:
Clear the chart?

· Local cache will be deleted.
· ESP32 datapoints will reload automatically if connected.
confirm_resetAll: Reset all configuration? This will remove all kilns and datapoints from this browser.
confirm_deleteKiln: Delete kiln "{name}"? This cannot be undone.
confirm_relayOn: Turn kiln ON? The relay will close and power the kiln.
confirm_relayOff: Turn kiln OFF? The relay will open and cut kiln power.
contact_lead: Have a question about Páramo, want to collaborate, or found a bug? Reach out — this space is reserved for the right words.
guide_lead: Páramo connects to your kiln monitor via an MQTT server. Here you'll find setup instructions, connection details, and usage notes. Full documentation for makers and technical users is on the
guide_lead_contact: If you have any doubts, feel free to use the
g_s1_desc: Páramo uses a Type S thermocouple for accurate high-temperature readings. See the
g_s1_desc2: for recommended models and wiring instructions.
g_s2_1_a: We chose this free MQTT server because it provides more than enough capacity without extra costs for the end user. Alternative self-hosted servers are possible for more technical users — see the
g_s2_1_c: create a free account, and set a username and password. You will also be given a cluster token. Save all three for the next step.
g_s2_2_b: as the notification provider for its simplicity and free pricing.
g_s2_2_c: Download the app on any device, then create a new topic. Give it a name that is not easily guessable — just for you.
g_s2_2_d: In the topic settings you will find the Topic URL. Save it for the next step.
g_s3_title: Turn on your Páramo Kiln Monitor
g_s3_desc_a: On first boot, the device creates a
g_s3_desc_b: Wi-Fi portal. Connect to it from any phone or laptop to configure your credentials.
g_s3_1: Choose your preferred Wi-Fi network and enter the password. We recommend the most stable network near where the device will live — close to the kiln.
g_s3_2: Enter the HiveMQ credentials you set up in step 2.1.
g_s3_3: Paste the NTFY topic URL you created in step 2.2.
g_s4_desc: Your Páramo Kiln Monitor now has everything it needs to communicate with you over the internet. You will receive temperature alerts and fault notifications directly on your device.
g_s5_1_b: On mobile, we suggest adding it to your home screen and using it as a web app.
g_s5_2: This app relies on local cache. If you clear it, some configurations will be lost. Export them first using the toolbar — they can be re-imported on any device.
g_s5_3_desc: On first open there is a blank "Studio Kiln" entry. Open the Connection panel under it and enter your HiveMQ credentials to link the device with the web app. You can rename or delete kilns using the buttons to the right, and add as many as you want — each one needs its own Páramo monitor.
g_s5_4_power: bridges an MQTT relay to cut kiln power remotely in case of emergency. See the
g_s5_4_power2: A solution for gas kilns is being studied.
g_s5_4_mode: shows a reference ramp curve alongside your actual firing for easy comparison, and tags the session for later search in the Journal.
g_s5_4_notes: write observations during or after a firing before exporting. Saved with the session in the Journal.
g_s5_4_export: copy your configuration to other devices or reset all stored data.
g_s5_4_journal: a searchable database of all your firings, local or cloud. The cloud function uses Google Drive for convenience. If you'd rather keep things off Google, just export your sessions to a folder and load them manually in the Journal. A self-hosted solution is being explored, balancing user friction against the limited resources of an individual project.
g_s5_4_cooldown: an alarm that triggers on the way down, once a firing has been detected and the kiln cools past a temperature you set. Useful to know when it is safe to start unloading. Adjustable from the Alarm Threshold card (150–550 °C).
g_s5_4_cone: note the Orton cone you are aiming for at the top of the Session card. It is purely a reference — it travels with the firing record and lets you search and group firings by cone in the Journal.
g_s5_4_ktype: choose your kiln type (electric, gas, wood or other) to control which power options are shown, or simply to tag the firing so you can filter by kiln type in the Journal.
g_s5_4_share: export any firing from the Journal as a printable greyscale image — chart, key data and notes laid out on an A4 sheet — to share with fellow potters or keep on paper.
g_s5_4_energy: set your price per kWh and currency to get a rough cost estimate per firing. This is an approximation only and should not be taken as a single source of truth — it cannot account for the complexity of a real electricity bill (tariff bands, standing charges, taxes, time-of-use rates).
g_s5_4_lang: the app is available in several languages, selectable from the toolbar. Missing yours, or spotted a translation that could be better? You can contribute one — see the
licence_lead: Páramo is released under copyleft licenses chosen to keep this project permanently open. Full details and license texts are on GitHub.
licence_block:
Firmware & Web Application
GNU Affero General Public License v3.0

Hardware (PCB · Schematics · BOM · 3D / STL)
CERN Open Hardware Licence Strongly Reciprocal v2.0

Documentation & Tutorials
Creative Commons Attribution-ShareAlike 4.0

These licenses are complementary. AGPL covers all software layers and any derived online services — if you run a modified version as a service, you must publish the source. CERN-OHL-S covers physical designs — if you distribute derived hardware, the schematics stay open.

No permissive licenses (MIT, Apache, BSD) were used. Deliberately.
Full license texts → github.com/ParamoStudio/KilnMonitor
licence_ethos: A kiln should not be firstly perceived as a consumer product. It is a tool built to last decades, passed between studios, repaired by hand, adapted to the specific needs of the person using it. The electronics and software we build around kilns should be no different. Any action towards encouraging the right to repair, understand, recycle and upgrade is a step towards what we deserve as communities. In any context given.
licence_ethos2: Páramo was built on open resources, community knowledge, and freely shared prior work. It will remain open in return. Copyleft licenses were chosen deliberately — not by default — to prevent proprietary capture of derivatives, whether as closed software forks, closed web services, or proprietary hardware products.
credits: For every single generous artist, coder, engineer, and maker that has taught, helped, or gifted — me or any other. Filling the well of abundant community knowledge without profit and selfishness as a main drive: Thanks. We are much. We are present. Open source is the way.
relayConfigNote: Device ID = MQTT Client ID set in the Ogemray's MQTT settings.
log_alarmOffDisconnected: Alarm off (not connected — set on ESP once connected)
log_newFiringEsp: New firing started — ESP32 history cleared
log_newFiringLocal: New firing started (local only — ESP32 not connected)
log_sleepOn: Páramo is in deep sleep mode. Turn on the kiln to activate live temperature data.
log_sleepOff: Kiln activity detected — live monitoring resumed.
log_relayCmd: Relay command sent — waiting for confirmation…
confirm_resetAll2: Are you absolutely sure? This cannot be undone.
noteSub: Add your personal notes — load, atmosphere, observations. Saved with the firing JSON.
noFiringsMsg: Load your exported JSON files to browse your firing history.
noMatchesMsg: Try adjusting or clearing the filters.
cooldown_note: Notifies once when the kiln cools past this point (150–550°C).
energyNote: Saved per firing — used in Journal to estimate cost.
wifi_modal_sub:
The kiln controller or the computer lost WiFi suddenly.
Please check your connection and reconnect.
fault_modal_sub:
Check thermocouple connection and wiring.
The kiln should not be operated until this is resolved.
shareEcoNote: * Rendered in greyscale to minimise ink, resources and ecological footprint. Care for the planet.
_section_fixes2: ── NEW: test-pass 2 (cone menu, mode editor placeholders) ──
coneNote: Reference only — used to tag and search firings in the Journal.
_section_fixes3: ── NEW: test-pass 3 (journal count, date/type filters, overlay) ──
